concerns.text: Task T004 defines HRF lag as a range '4-6s' and T016 references '4-6s lag'. The Constitution (Principle I) and Spec (FR-002) require deterministic reproducibility. A code constant cannot be a range; this task silently relaxes the requirement for a fixed, reproducible HRF model (e.g., double-gamma with specific parameters) mandated by the spec's 'canonical HRF' requirement.

Task T032 mandates implementing 'Subject-level AR(1) pre-whitened GLM + Group-level t-test'. The Spec (FR-004, US-3) only mandates 'linear regression' with FDR and permutation tests. The 'Group-level t-test' and 'AR(1) pre-whitening' are scope additions not authorized by the spec, violating the constraint that tasks must not introduce unverified methodological complexity (Constitution Principle II).

Tasks T004, T005, and T014 hardcode the dataset ID 'ds000248'. The Spec (FR-001, US-1) and Constitution (Principle VI) explicitly reference 'ds000246' (or equivalent). This is a silent drift in the primary data source, violating the Constitution's requirement that the exact version/snapshot be recorded and matched to the spec's methodology.

Task T035 requires outputting 'is_significant' in the JSON. While Spec SC-001 mentions this, the Spec's Functional Requirements (FR-004/FR-005) and User Story 3 acceptance criteria only mandate generating the null distribution and confirming if the result falls outside the 95% CI. The task assumes a boolean derivation logic not explicitly defined in the FRs, risking a silent relaxation of the 'how' to determine significance.
